name: Cauliflower with Beef "Szechwan Style
recipeIngredient: 2 Carrots, medium, cut into 1/2 inch cubes
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the round steak into 1/2 inch cubes.
2. Make a thin paste of cornstarch for thickening.
Combine soy and sherry for marinade. Marinate the steak pieces for 1 hour, massaging the meat in the marinade to aid tenderizing. Drain and reserve the marinade.
3. Wash and soak the mushrooms for 1 hour, then drain and thinly slice.
4. Wash, peel, and roll-cut the carrots (slice on a slight bias into 1-inch lengths, turning the carrot a quarter turn between slices).
5. Wash cauliflower, trim off thick stems, and cut into pieces about the size of the carrot pieces.
6. Parboil the carrots in boiling stock for about 3 minutes or until just beginning to soften. Reserve the carrots and stock.
7. Stir-frying: Heat peanut oil in a hot wok. When the oil just begins to smoke, add the drained steak cubes; stir-fry briskly for 1 minute until the meat begins to lose its pinkness. Do not overcook the meat or it will become tough. Remove to a holding plate.
8. Swirl the remaining oil into the wok. Add ginger, garlic, and peppercorns; stir-fry for 15 seconds.
9. Add cauliflower and mushrooms; stir-fry for 1 minute.
10. Add reserved carrot stock, marinade, and mushroom soaking liquid. Bring to a boil.
11. Reduce heat to medium, cover the wok, and simmer for 3 minutes.
12. Remove the lid, turn the heat to high, and when the sauce boils again, add carrots and beef.
13. Mix together. Splash vinegar down the sides of the wok.
14. Push ingredients out of the sauce; thicken with the cornstarch paste. The sauce should be a light gravy.
15. Add sesame oil and toss in green onions. Serve.
